concepts.name: Unit
concepts.description: The number one; the fundamental building block of all numbers.

concepts.name: Number
concepts.description: A multitude composed of units.

concepts.name: Counting
concepts.description: The process of determining the number of elements in a set.

concepts.name: Natural Numbers
concepts.description: The counting numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, ...

concepts.name: Integers
concepts.description: Whole numbers including negatives: ..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...

concepts.name: Rational Numbers
concepts.description: Numbers expressible as a ratio p/q of two integers where q ≠ 0. Includes fractions and terminating/repeating decimals.

concepts.name: Real Numbers
concepts.description: All rational and irrational numbers; every point on the number line. Includes √2, π, e, and all decimals.

concepts.name: Complex Numbers
concepts.description: Numbers of the form a + bi where i² = −1. Extend the reals to solve all polynomial equations (Fundamental Theorem of Algebra).

concepts.name: Place Value
concepts.description: The value of a digit based on its position in a number.

concepts.name: Number Systems (Bases)
concepts.description: Different bases for representing numbers: binary (2), octal (8), decimal (10), hexadecimal (16).

concepts.name: Divisibility
concepts.description: When one number divides another without remainder.

concepts.name: Prime Numbers
concepts.description: Numbers greater than 1 with exactly two divisors: 1 and itself. The atoms of arithmetic.

concepts.name: Number Theory
concepts.description: The study of integers and their properties: primes, divisibility, congruences, and Diophantine equations.

concepts.name: Addition
concepts.description: Combining two quantities to get a total. The most fundamental arithmetic operation from which all others are built.

concepts.name: Subtraction
concepts.description: Finding the difference between two quantities; the inverse of addition.

concepts.name: Multiplication
concepts.description: Repeated addition; scaling one quantity by another.

concepts.name: Division
concepts.description: Splitting a quantity into equal parts; the inverse of multiplication. Leads to fractions and the division algorithm.

concepts.name: Fractions
concepts.description: Numbers expressed as a/b representing parts of a whole. Foundation for ratios, proportions, and rational numbers.

concepts.name: Ratio & Proportion
concepts.description: A ratio compares two quantities; a proportion states two ratios are equal. Central to similarity, scaling, and musical intervals.

concepts.name: Percentage
concepts.description: A ratio expressed as parts per hundred. Used universally in finance, statistics, and everyday comparisons.

concepts.name: Exponents & Powers
concepts.description: Repeated multiplication: aⁿ = a × a × ... × a (n times). Foundation for exponential growth, logarithms, and polynomial algebra.

concepts.name: Logarithms
concepts.description: The inverse of exponentiation: log_b(x) = y means b^y = x. Converts multiplication to addition; foundational in information theory.

concepts.name: Average (Mean, Median, Mode)
concepts.description: Measures of central tendency: mean (sum/count), median (middle value), mode (most frequent).

concepts.name: Modular Arithmetic
concepts.description: Arithmetic of remainders: a ≡ b (mod n) means n divides (a − b). Clock arithmetic, cryptography, error detection.

concepts.name: Set Theory
concepts.description: The study of collections of objects: union, intersection, complement. The language underlying all of modern mathematics.

concepts.name: Mathematical Logic
concepts.description: Formal reasoning: propositions, connectives (and, or, not, implies), quantifiers (for all, there exists), and proof methods.

concepts.name: Axioms & Proof
concepts.description: Self-evident starting truths from which theorems are derived. Euclid's five postulates defined geometry; ZFC axioms define modern set theory.

concepts.name: Methods of Proof
concepts.description: Direct proof, proof by contradiction, proof by induction, and contrapositive — the tools for establishing mathematical truth.

concepts.name: Point
concepts.description: That which has no part; a location with no size.

concepts.name: Line
concepts.description: Breadthless length; extends infinitely in both directions.

concepts.name: Angle
concepts.description: The inclination of two lines meeting at a point, measured in degrees or radians.

concepts.name: Right Angle
concepts.description: An angle of 90 degrees; when a line stands on another making equal adjacent angles.

concepts.name: Triangle
concepts.description: A polygon with three sides and three angles. The simplest polygon and building block of all geometry.

concepts.name: Right Triangle
concepts.description: A triangle with one right angle — the foundation of trigonometry and the Pythagorean theorem.

concepts.name: Area
concepts.description: The measure of the size of a two-dimensional surface.

concepts.name: Square
concepts.description: A rectangle with four equal sides and four right angles.

concepts.name: Circle
concepts.description: The set of all points equidistant from a center. Circumference = 2πr, Area = πr².

concepts.name: Pi (π)
concepts.description: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, approximately 3.14159. Irrational and transcendental.

concepts.name: Polygons
concepts.description: Closed plane figures with straight sides: quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and beyond.

concepts.name: Geometric Transformations
concepts.description: Translation, rotation, reflection, and dilation — operations that move or resize figures while preserving or scaling properties.

concepts.name: Symmetry
concepts.description: Invariance under transformation. Reflective, rotational, and translational symmetry connect geometry to group theory.

concepts.name: Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.description: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c² where c is the hypotenuse. Over 370 known proofs.

concepts.name: Number Line
concepts.description: A visual representation of numbers on a straight line, bridging arithmetic and geometry.

concepts.name: Coordinate Plane
concepts.description: The Cartesian plane: two perpendicular number lines creating an x-y grid for plotting points, lines, and curves.

concepts.name: Slope
concepts.description: The steepness of a line: rise/run = (y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁). Foreshadows the derivative in calculus.

concepts.name: Variables & Expressions
concepts.description: Letters representing unknown quantities. Algebraic expressions combine variables with operations.

concepts.name: Equations
concepts.description: Statements that two expressions are equal. Solving means finding the values that make the equation true.

concepts.name: Linear Equations
concepts.description: Equations of degree 1: y = mx + b. Their graphs are straight lines; solving systems finds intersection points.

concepts.name: Function
concepts.description: A relation that assigns exactly one output to each input. The central object of modern mathematics.

concepts.name: Inverse Function
concepts.description: A function that reverses another: if f(a) = b then f⁻¹(b) = a. Exists when f is one-to-one.

concepts.name: Polynomial
concepts.description: An expression of variables and coefficients with addition, subtraction, and non-negative integer exponents.

concepts.name: Quadratic Function
concepts.description: A polynomial of degree 2: f(x) = ax² + bx + c. Roots given by the quadratic formula.

concepts.name: Parabola
concepts.description: The graph of a quadratic function; a U-shaped curve. Also a conic section.

concepts.name: Sequences & Series
concepts.description: Ordered lists of numbers following a rule. Arithmetic, geometric, and Fibonacci sequences; convergent and divergent series.

concepts.name: Infinity
concepts.description: The concept of unboundedness. Cantor proved some infinities are larger than others; connects to limits, series, and set theory.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Ratios
concepts.description: sin, cos, tan — ratios of sides in a right triangle. Extend to the unit circle for all angles.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Identities
concepts.description: Fundamental relationships: sin²θ + cos²θ = 1, addition formulas, double-angle formulas.

concepts.name: Polar Coordinates
concepts.description: Representing points as (r, θ) — distance from origin and angle. Natural for circular and spiral shapes.

concepts.name: Limits
concepts.description: The value a function approaches as input approaches some value. The foundation on which all of calculus is built.

concepts.name: Derivatives
concepts.description: The instantaneous rate of change: f′(x) = lim[f(x+h)−f(x)]/h. Slope of tangent line; velocity from position.

concepts.name: Integrals
concepts.description: Accumulation of quantities: area under a curve. The Fundamental Theorem connects integrals to derivatives.

concepts.name: Differential Equations
concepts.description: Equations relating a function to its derivatives. Model growth, decay, oscillation, and virtually all physical phenomena.

concepts.name: Taylor Series
concepts.description: Approximating any smooth function by a polynomial: f(x) ≈ Σ f⁽ⁿ⁾(a)(x−a)ⁿ/n!. Powers calculators and numerical methods.

concepts.name: Riemann Sums & Numerical Integration
concepts.description: Approximating definite integrals by summing rectangular areas; the conceptual foundation of the integral.

concepts.name: Calculus Optimization
concepts.description: Using derivatives to find maxima and minima: set f′(x) = 0, check f″(x). The basis of engineering design and ML.

concepts.name: Calculus in Physics: Motion & Forces
concepts.description: Position → velocity → acceleration via derivatives; F = ma as an ODE; projectile trajectories via integration.

concepts.name: Vectors
concepts.description: Quantities with magnitude and direction. Addition, scaling, dot product, cross product. Foundation of physics and linear algebra.

concepts.name: Matrices
concepts.description: Rectangular arrays of numbers representing linear transformations. Multiplication, inverse, determinant.

concepts.name: Linear Algebra
concepts.description: The study of vector spaces and linear maps: spans, bases, rank, null space. The language of ML, graphics, and quantum mechanics.

concepts.name: Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors
concepts.description: Av = λv — vectors unchanged in direction by a transformation. Govern stability, principal components, and quantum states.

concepts.name: Singular Value Decomposition
concepts.description: A = UΣVᵀ — every matrix factors into rotation, scaling, rotation. The Swiss army knife of data science.

concepts.name: Probability
concepts.description: Measuring the likelihood of events: P(A) = favorable outcomes / total outcomes. Axioms, conditional probability, Bayes' theorem.

concepts.name: Combinatorics
concepts.description: Counting arrangements: permutations (order matters), combinations (order doesn't). Pascal's triangle, binomial theorem.

concepts.name: Statistics
concepts.description: Collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data. Distributions, hypothesis testing, regression, confidence intervals.

concepts.name: Binomial Theorem
concepts.description: (a+b)ⁿ = Σ C(n,k) aⁿ⁻ᵏ bᵏ — expanding powers using Pascal's triangle. Connects combinatorics and algebra.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.description: P(A|B) = P(B|A)·P(A)/P(B) — updating beliefs with evidence. Foundation of Bayesian inference and spam filters.

concepts.name: Graph Theory
concepts.description: The study of vertices and edges: paths, cycles, trees, connectivity. Models networks, maps, and social connections.

concepts.name: Game Theory
concepts.description: Mathematical modeling of strategic interaction. Nash equilibrium, prisoner's dilemma, minimax theorem.

concepts.name: Cryptography
concepts.description: Securing communication using mathematics: RSA (prime factoring), Diffie-Hellman (discrete log), AES, elliptic curves.

concepts.name: Optimization
concepts.description: Finding the best solution: linear programming, gradient descent, convex optimization. The engine of ML and operations research.

concepts.name: Topology
concepts.description: The study of properties preserved under continuous deformation: connectedness, compactness, genus. A donut = coffee mug.

concepts.name: Fractals
concepts.description: Self-similar structures with fractional dimension: Mandelbrot set, Koch snowflake, Sierpiński triangle.

concepts.name: Chaos Theory
concepts.description: Deterministic systems with sensitive dependence on initial conditions. Lyapunov exponents, strange attractors, the butterfly effect.

concepts.name: Neural Network Mathematics
concepts.description: Weighted sums, activation functions, and universal approximation. The mathematical framework for deep learning.

concepts.name: Backpropagation
concepts.description: Computing gradients via the chain rule through a computational graph. The algorithm that makes deep learning trainable.

concepts.name: Loss Functions
concepts.description: Quantifying prediction error: MSE, cross-entropy, hinge loss. The objective that training minimizes.

concepts.name: Attention Mechanism
concepts.description: Softmax(QKᵀ/√d)V — learned relevance weighting. The core of Transformers, enabling context-aware processing.

concepts.name: Transformers
concepts.description: Multi-head self-attention + feedforward layers. The architecture behind GPT, BERT, and modern AI.

concepts.name: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
concepts.description: Fine-tuning large models by learning low-rank weight updates ΔW = BA, reducing trainable parameters by 100–1000×.

concepts.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
concepts.description: Combining vector-similarity search with language generation. Reduces hallucination by grounding answers in retrieved documents.

concepts.name: Dimensionality Reduction
concepts.description: PCA, t-SNE, UMAP — projecting high-dimensional data to fewer dimensions while preserving structure.

concepts.name: Harmonic Series in Music
concepts.description: Integer multiples of a fundamental frequency that determine timbre and consonance.

concepts.name: Pythagorean Tuning
concepts.description: A tuning system based on stacking pure fifths (3:2 ratio). Reveals the Pythagorean comma.

concepts.name: Fibonacci & Golden Ratio in Music
concepts.description: Fibonacci numbers in the chromatic scale (13, 8, 5, 3, 2) and golden ratio in composition structure.

concepts.name: Mozart's Musical Dice Game
concepts.description: A combinatorial composition system where two dice select from 176 pre-composed measures to generate 11^16 unique minuets.

concepts.name: Euclidean Rhythms
concepts.description: Distributing k beats among n steps using Euclid's algorithm produces rhythms found across world music traditions.

concepts.name: Group Theory in Music
concepts.description: Musical transformations (transposition, inversion, retrograde) form algebraic groups.

concepts.name: Fractal Music & Self-Similarity
concepts.description: Self-similar structures in music, from Bach's canons to algorithmic composition using L-systems and 1/f noise.

concepts.name: Wave Equation & Acoustics
concepts.description: The PDE governing sound propagation, standing waves, and resonant frequencies of musical instruments.

concepts.name: Fast Fourier Transform
concepts.description: The Cooley-Tukey algorithm computes the DFT in O(N log N) via divide-and-conquer, exploiting roots of unity symmetry.

concepts.name: Inverse FFT & Signal Reconstruction
concepts.description: Recovering a time-domain signal from its frequency representation using the unitarity of the DFT matrix.

concepts.name: Frequency Domain Filtering
concepts.description: Filtering via the convolution theorem: multiply spectra in the frequency domain instead of convolving in time.

concepts.name: 2D FFT & Image Processing
concepts.description: The separable 2D DFT decomposes images into spatial frequencies for edge detection, denoising, and compression.

concepts.name: Fourier Transform & Image Compression
concepts.description: DCT-based compression (JPEG) and the Fourier Slice Theorem (CT/MRI reconstruction).
